skill.name: ui
skill.description: Helps generate frontend UI components and feedback forms with responsive layout and accessibility guidance.
skill.summary: Create UI components and feedback forms with accessibility checks.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: The high static findings were reviewed and dismissed as false positives. The reported weak cryptography and obfuscation hits are front matter or Japanese prose, and the reported command execution hits are Markdown fences. The remaining concern is low risk: the skill allows Bash even though the reviewed instructions do not require shell commands.

low_findings.title: Unnecessary Bash Tool Permission
low_findings.description: The skill front matter allows Bash in all reviewed skill files. The instructions describe UI generation and do not require specific shell commands. No command execution, network call, or secret access instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission is explicit in front matter, but no executable commands are present. This supports a low-risk permission concern rather than confirmed misuse.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm hits point to descriptions and accessibility text, not cryptographic code. No hash, cipher, password handling, or security-sensitive crypto use was found at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language descriptions or accessibility guidance. There is no semantic evidence of a weak cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The entropy alerts are caused by front matter and Japanese text. No encoded payload, binary content, prompt injection, or hidden execution instruction was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious entropy locations are readable Markdown or YAML. The files contain no opaque blob, decoder, or hidden payload behavior.

low_findings.title: Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences around checklists. They are not executable Ruby, shell substitutions, or command strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are Markdown fence delimiters around documentation. There is no interpreter context or user-controlled execution path.

content.value_statement: Teams often need consistent UI components without rebuilding common patterns from scratch. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create responsive components and feedback forms.
actual_capabilities: Routes component requests to component-specific instructions.
Routes feedback form requests to feedback-specific instructions.
Supports hero sections, pricing tables, feature sections, contact sections, footers, and navigation.
Applies accessibility checklist guidance for labels, focus states, alt text, and contrast.
Guides creation of feedback forms, bug report forms, feature requests, and satisfaction surveys.
limitations: It provides guidance, not a complete design system.
It does not define backend authentication or database behavior.
It may require project-specific styling decisions from the user.
It declares Bash access, although no shell command is required by the instructions.

use_cases.title: Create marketing sections
use_cases.description: Generate hero, feature, pricing, contact, header, and footer sections for a product page.

use_cases.title: Add user feedback flows
use_cases.description: Create forms for feedback, bug reports, feature requests, or satisfaction surveys.

use_cases.title: Improve accessibility coverage
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to add labels, keyboard support, focus states, alt text, and contrast checks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic section
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a responsive hero section for my app using the existing project style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one common UI component.

prompt_templates.title: Build a feedback form
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a feedback form with fields for message, rating, category, and contact email.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a component set
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a landing page set with hero, features, pricing, contact, and footer sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for related page components.

prompt_templates.title: Apply accessibility requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the generated UI for labels, keyboard navigation, focus states, alt text, contrast, and screen reader support.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for quality review after implementation.

output_examples.input: Create a pricing section for a SaaS page.
output_examples.output: A responsive pricing layout with plan names, prices, features, clear action buttons, and accessible labels.

output_examples.input: Add a bug report form to my app.
output_examples.output: A form design with issue type, description, severity, contact details, validation states, and submission guidance.

output_examples.input: Check this contact form for accessibility.
output_examples.output: A concise review covering labels, focus order, keyboard behavior, error messages, contrast, and screen reader announcements.

best_practices: Provide the target framework, style system, and component location.
Ask for accessibility checks before accepting generated UI.
Define feedback categories and storage expectations before implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for authentication, backend business logic, or database design.
Do not request vague UI without content, layout goals, or target users.
Do not skip manual review of generated forms that collect user data.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps create frontend UI components and feedback forms.

faq.question: Does it support accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes checklist guidance for labels, focus states, alt text, contrast, and keyboard use.

faq.question: Can it build backend services?
faq.answer: No. The skill is intended for UI and form guidance, not backend business logic.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed static findings were false positives, with one low-risk Bash permission concern.

faq.question: What should users provide?
faq.answer: Users should provide the target component, framework, style rules, content needs, and accessibility expectations.
